commands.review_summary:
The PR adds provider-aware Codex app-server approval reviewer selection and applies it to run attempts, side-question forks, and bound conversation turns.

PR surface: Source +66, Tests +370. Total +436 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main can send `auto_review` for exec auto before model-provider scoping, and the PR's remaining requirements edge is also source-reproducible. I did not run a live LM Studio/Ollama round trip.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR is draft, maintainer-protected, and needs owner acceptance plus a provider/requirements repair before it is a clean landing candidate.

target.title: fix(codex): avoid guardian review for local models
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Prevents Codex app-server Guardian / `auto_review` from being used as the approval reviewer when the active model provider is local or custom, such as LM Studio.
- Keeps `tools.exec.mode=auto` guarded for local/custom providers by preserving prompting approvals and sandboxing while routing the review to a human.
- Applies the same provider-aware reviewer policy to normal Codex app-server runs, bound conversation turns, and side-question forks.

Why does this matter now?

- `tools.exec.mode=auto` forced Codex Guardian before checking the selected model provider. A local model routed through the Codex harness could therefore be treated as the safety reviewer.

What is the intended outcome?

- Codex-native and OpenAI-backed Codex app-server runs can keep model-backed approval review.
- Local/custom providers fall back to `approvalsReviewer: "user"` while retaining `approvalPolicy: "on-request"` and workspace sandboxing.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to generic OpenClaw exec auto-reviewer model selection.
- No changes to Codex app-server protocol fields or provider catalog configuration.

What does success look like?

- `lmstudio` / local provider Codex runs no longer send `approvalsReviewer: "auto_review"` or `guardian_subagent`.
- Existing provider-qualified legacy bindings such as `lmstudio/local-model` are handled safely even without a stored `modelProvider`.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The provider gate in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts`.
- The three request-shaping call sites in run attempts, conversation bindings, and side-question forks.
- Whether preserving `on-request` + sandbox while downgrading only the reviewer is the right compatibility boundary.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Maintainer-requested investigation and fix during local debugging.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: local/custom Codex harness model providers no longer use Codex Guardian / model-backed approval review for exec auto mode.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree on the Codex plugin test harness, plus Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ksz35f9ead867j30fk2awkca` against the pushed PR head.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`; `node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --id tbx_01ksz35f9ead867j30fk2awkca --reclaim --keep --shell -- "set -euo pipefail; env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=true corepack pnpm check:changed; env CI=true corepack pnpm test:changed; env CI=true OPENCLAW_TEST_CHANGED_BROAD=1 corepack pnpm test:changed"`.
- Evidence after fix: local focused Vitest reported 4 passed files / 135 passed tests; local oxfmt, oxlint, and `git diff --check` passed; Testbox `check:changed` and `test:changed` passed with the changed Codex shard reporting 4 passed files / 135 passed tests.
- Observed result after fix: mocked `lmstudio` Codex app-server start/turn, code-mode-only, side-question, and bound-conversation flows keep prompting approval and sandboxing but send `approvalsReviewer: "user"`; Codex/OpenAI providers still keep `auto_review`.
- What was not tested: a live LM Studio or Ollama app-server round trip with a real model process.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: full `pnpm test extensions/codex` was not treated as a PR gate because the same Blacksmith Testbox reproduces current `origin/main` failures in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.test.ts` for legacy auth-provider alias handling. The PR-touched files and changed-gate proof pass.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing config resolution forced `tools.exec.mode=auto` into Codex app-server `approvalsReviewer: "auto_review"` before considering the selected model provider.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --id tbx_01ksz35f9ead867j30fk2awkca --reclaim --keep --shell -- "set -euo pipefail; env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=true corepack pnpm check:changed; env CI=true corepack pnpm test:changed; env CI=true OPENCLAW_TEST_CHANGED_BROAD=1 corepack pnpm test:changed"`
- Autoreview with Codex reviewer against `origin/main`; final result: no accepted/actionable findings.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Policy-level coverage for Codex/OpenAI preserving model-backed review and custom providers downgrading to user review.
- Run-attempt coverage for `lmstudio` + `tools.exec.mode=auto` request shaping.
- Bound-conversation coverage for stored custom `modelProvider` and legacy provider-qualified model refs.
- Native code-mode-only coverage for provider-qualified local Codex model refs on main turns and side-question forks.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The runtime policy was selected before model-provider scoping, so `tools.exec.mode=auto` could send model-backed Guardian review for non-OpenAI model providers.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Approval routing for Codex app-server exec auto mode when a run uses provider overrides or existing bound-session metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change narrows only model-backed approval review. It preserves prompting approval policy and sandbox posture for custom providers, and keeps Codex/OpenAI behavior unchanged.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Live local-model app-server proof was not run.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Autoreview completed with no accepted/actionable findings.

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the code path and kept the change scoped to Codex app-server approval reviewer routing.
target.author: vincentkoc
